title: BITE Model Assessment Quiz
attribution: Based on Steven Hassan's BITE Model of Undue Influence
disclaimer: This quiz is educational and not diagnostic. Scoring does not diagnose cult involvement. Consult a mental health professional for concerns about undue influence.

behavior.name: Behavior Control
behavior.description: Control over physical actions, finances, dress, diet, sleep, and daily activities

questions.text: Are members expected to dress in specific ways or follow strict appearance codes?
Does the group control what you eat, drink, or consume?
Are financial decisions made by leadership rather than individuals?
Is there pressure to give money, possessions, or property to the group?
Are members required to live in communal housing?
Is there control over sleep, work schedules, or free time?
Are relationships and marriages arranged or heavily influenced by leadership?
Is there punishment for breaking rules or not following directives?
Are members monitored or tracked regarding their activities?
Is participation in group activities mandatory or strongly coerced?
Must members ask permission for major life decisions?
Is sexual behavior controlled or restricted by the group?
Are children required to engage in adult group activities?
Must members separate from family members who don't approve?
Is there control over who you can spend time with or befriend?
Are members required to participate in public rituals or ceremonies?
Must members work for the group without fair compensation?
Is there prohibition on using modern technology or outside media?
Are members punished for expressing doubts or questioning practices?
Is leaving the group made practically or emotionally difficult?

information.name: Information Control
information.description: Control over information, media access, education, and critical thinking

questions.text: Are members discouraged from reading outside materials or non-group sources?
Does the group restrict access to news, internet, or media?
Is the group portrayed as the only source of truth?
Are members taught not to trust outside experts or authority figures?
Are criticisms or negative information about the group forbidden?
Does the group use special language or terminology outsiders don't understand?
Is critical thinking or asking questions discouraged?
Are members given different information based on their rank or tenure?
Is education redirected away from secular subjects to group doctrine?
Are members taught that the outside world is dangerous or corrupt?
Is doubt presented as a personal moral failing?
Are historical facts rewritten or reinterpreted by the group?
Is there pressure to accept group beliefs without evidence or logic?
Are scientific findings contradicted by group teachings?
Must members defend group teachings to non-members?
Are members taught that they cannot understand the group's teachings alone?
Is confession of doubts used to reinforce group loyalty?
Are members discouraged from pursuing higher education outside the group?
Is information about finances, decisions, or practices kept secret?
Are members expected to spy on or report other members' thoughts?

thought.name: Thought Control
thought.description: Control over beliefs, decision-making, and internal mental processes

questions.text: Are members required to believe specific doctrines without question?
Is doubt about group teachings considered a moral or spiritual failure?
Are personal experiences reinterpreted to fit group ideology?
Is black-and-white thinking encouraged (us vs. them, good vs. evil)?
Must decisions be made based on group guidance rather than individual judgment?
Are members discouraged from pursuing personal interests or ambitions?
Is meditation, hypnosis, or altered consciousness used for indoctrination?
Are members taught that staying in the group is the only path to safety or salvation?
Is independent thinking framed as selfish or prideful?
Are paradoxes or contradictions explained away rather than addressed?
Is fear used to maintain obedience and belief?
Are members taught to suppress their own intuition or instincts?
Is the group's purpose presented as the only meaningful thing in life?
Must members filter all information through group interpretations?
Are prophecies or predictions used to maintain belief?
Is leaving the group portrayed as resulting in disaster or punishment?
Are members taught that opposing thoughts are demonic or evil?
Is special knowledge or revelation claimed by the leadership only?
Must members rationalize their doubts rather than address them?
Is the group's philosophy presented as scientifically proven?

emotional.name: Emotional Control
emotional.description: Manipulation of emotions, identity, and psychological states

questions.text: Are members made to feel special or chosen compared to outsiders?
Is there manipulation through guilt or shame?
Are relationships used as leverage to maintain compliance?
Is leaving the group used as a threat to control behavior?
Are members' fears used to increase dependence on the group?
Is identity merged with group membership?
Are members taught to fear abandoning the group?
Is love or approval withheld when members disobey?
Are emotional crises or vulnerabilities exploited for recruitment or control?
Does leadership control your sense of what is real or true?
Are members emotionally dependent on the leader or group?
Is there punishment through isolation, shunning, or excommunication?
Are members taught that their worth depends on their loyalty?
Does the group use intermittent reinforcement (unpredictable rewards/punishments)?
Are members required to confess personal information used for control?
Is happiness or contentment only possible within the group?
Are members made to feel responsible for the group's well-being?
Does the group manufacture crises to increase emotional bonding?
Is love presented conditionally on adherence to group rules?
Are members' personal relationships sacrificed for group loyalty?

scoringBands.description: Minimal signs of undue influence. Group appears relatively healthy.
Some concerning control patterns. Warrant further investigation and caution.
Significant control dynamics present. Consistent with high-control group characteristics.
Extensive undue influence across multiple domains. Strong indicators of cult-like control.
